title: Sunrun Inc (RUN) Q1 2024 Earnings Highlights
text: Sunrun exceeded installation guidance in Q1 2024 with storage capacity reaching 207 megawatt hours—nearly triple prior-year period. The company installed 177 megawatts of solar energy capacity, surpassing expectations. Storage attachment rates improved significantly, reaching 50% for new customers, up from 15% previous year, demonstrating strong market demand for battery integration with solar installations. The company added approximately 22,000 subscribers in Q1, bringing total customer base to roughly 957,000 by quarter-end. Annual recurring revenue exceeded $1.4 billion, representing 30% year-over-year growth. Net subscriber value stood at $11,891 per subscriber with gross earning assets reaching $15 billion. The company reported negative cash generation of $311 million in Q1, though adjusted figures excluding one-time costs showed positive $6 million cash flow. Management lowered full-year solar installation guidance to down 15% to flat year-over-year, adjusting from prior expectations of down 5% to up 5%, reflecting slower-than-expected sales growth with direct business showing only 13% sequential expansion. For storage, the company projects 800 megawatt hours to 1 gigawatt hour annually. Management expects positive quarterly cash generation for remainder of 2024, with Q4 potentially reaching $200 million to $500 million annualized rates.
